title: Hair Stylist

rationales.taskAutomation: Cutting, coloring, and styling require fine motor skill and real-time adaptation to a client's head and hair; almost none of it is end-to-end automatable.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI styling-preview and virtual try-on apps exist and are improving, but no tool performs the physical service itself.
rationales.adoption: A minority of chain salons use AI consultation or booking tools; most independent stylists use none.
rationales.agenticExposure: Booking, reminders, and color-formula recordkeeping are agent-ownable; the haircut itself is not.

tasksAtRisk: Retail product recommendation based on hair profile
tasksGrowing: Real-time client consultation and style adaptation
Building a personal client book and referral base
salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 39-5012, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: Advanced cutting and color technique specialization
AI virtual try-on and style-consultation tools

insight: Virtual try-on apps can preview a haircut; no product can perform one. The stylist's exposure is entirely in scheduling and formula recordkeeping, not the chair.
